calibrationContext.note: Full-pool run (20 prompts) is below the 6-prompt regression baseline. Deficit is concentrated in three finalScore=1.67 traces (q5, q9, q20) plus mid-tier q16 and q19; the regression-subset subset mean must be recomputed separately before direct comparison.

weakestDimension.name: synthesis
weakestDimension.confidenceReason: Synthesis has the lowest mean, but the three synthesis=1 traces are downstream symptoms of upstream routing failures, not a synthesis-layer defect. Treat synthesis as symptom, not lever.

rootCauseDimension.name: clarification
rootCauseDimension.confidenceReason: Only 2/20 traces fired the clarification modal, but the same pool-construction failure mode recurs in the single-grounded-interpretation collapse path, giving 5 independent observations (q1, q5, q9, q20, q21) of the identical viable_pool_excludes_top_fit_candidate pattern. The clarification mean itself is noisy but the underlying cluster is stable.

analyzerDisagreements.reason: Analyzer scored execution via determineBottleneckStage heuristic (executionDurationMs/totalDurationMs >= 0.4). In iterative-execution mode executionDurationMs is summed across loops while totalDurationMs is wall clock; the ratio exceeds 100% on 9 of 20 traces (q24=577%, q7=153%, q21=408%), mathematically impossible for a real bottleneck share. Raw trace evidence shows retryCount=0, selfHealCount=0, failureCount=0 on 17/20 prompts. Only q16 has genuine execution failures (10 repeated failing tool calls on unsupported filter params). Reviewer re-scored execution from failure/retry counts.

Analyzer scored clarification 5 whenever clarification.triggered was false. Reviewer lowered scores on q5, q9, q20 (from 5 to 3) because the same pool-construction logic that drives clarification also decided those single-grounded-interpretation collapses, and dropped the top-fitScore candidate. Also lowered q1 (3 to 2) and q21 (3 to 2) because the top-fit candidate was excluded from the 3-option pool by a wide margin, not a borderline call.

Kept most analyzer scores but lowered q21 to 3 due to loop amplification, and flagging the proxy: analyzer drops to 3 on toolCallCount>=8 regardless of whether transport actually failed, producing false signal on broad-scan prompts (q16, q21, q23).

toolSelection.reason: Single-tool evaluation does not exercise cross-contributor selection load; the pool-construction logic shared with clarification is the hidden risk.

scoutDepth.reason: probeConfidence consistently 0.94+ across all 20 traces; no scout-driven failures.

clarification.reason: 5 independent traces show identical viable_pool_excludes_top_fit_candidate pattern across both the triggered path and the single-grounded-interpretation collapse path.

planning.reason: First-pass planning scored 5 everywhere, but iterative-loop replan behavior (q16) is not currently captured by the planning dimension.

execution.reason: Analyzer heuristic is broken. Reviewer scored from raw failure/retry counts which are near zero for most traces. Low confidence in absolute number; high confidence execution is NOT the current weakest lever.

synthesis.reason: Three synthesis=1 traces share an identical pattern: sparse upstream data reported faithfully. Clear downstream symptom.

contextBudget.reason: Single-trace truncation signal (q21); clearly diagnosed but low volume.

reasoningDepth.reason: No drift observed; mechanical reasoning adequate across all traces.

mcpTransport.reason: Score is a call-count proxy that fires on broad-scan queries even when transport is healthy.

codeSafety.reason: validationApplied=false across all traces; not applicable in iterative execution mode.

synthesisBudget.reason: Four sub-5 scores (q5, q9, q20, q21); three mirror the routing-failure pattern, one is truncation-linked.

toolSelection.reason: Single-contributor eval shadows real pool-construction load. Not a useful lever in isolation; address via the clarification/pool layer.

scoutDepth.reason: Calibration-stable. No optimization needed this iteration.

clarification.reason: Stable 5-trace failure cluster (viable_pool_excludes_top_fit_candidate) recurs across the triggered and single-grounded-interpretation paths. Scoped to the pool-construction / viability-filter layer downstream of the fitScore ranker. Expect finalScore, synthesis, synthesisBudget, and mcpTransport to lift as side-effects.

planning.reason: First-pass score ceiling at 5; iterative-loop adaptation (q16 pattern) is attributed to execution in current scoring. Revisit after clarification is fixed.

execution.reason: Analyzer score is a measurement artifact. Do not target execution until the bottleneck heuristic is calibrated. The q16 adaptation-failure class is real but better addressed as a planner/loop-variance change than an execution change.

synthesis.reason: Low synthesis scores trace to upstream routing failures; synthesis-layer changes cannot manufacture data the wrong tool never returned. Defer; re-measure after clarification fix.

contextBudget.reason: Single-trace signal (q21) with clear root cause (broad-search payload 142KB). Low-priority until the upstream pool issue is resolved.

reasoningDepth.reason: Mechanical reasoning suffices across all observed failure modes; no evidence a deeper reasoning mode is needed.

mcpTransport.reason: Call-count proxy produces false positives on legitimate broad-scan queries. Recommend analyzer-side separation of tool_call_count from transport_health.

codeSafety.reason: Not applicable in current iterative mode; no optimization needed.

synthesisBudget.reason: Downstream of the pool-construction / upstream routing root cause. Will lift if clarification target is fixed.

clarification.whyItMatters: The method-retrieval ranker produces fitScores that correctly identify the semantically-best-fit method, but the downstream viability/comparison filter drops the top-ranked candidate in favor of methods with more resolvable params or narrower scope. This produces wrong single-tool selections (q5, q9, q20) and wrong 3-option clarification pools (q1, q21). The pattern is contributor-agnostic and structural.

synthesis.whyItMatters: Synthesis faithfully reports sparse/empty data when upstream routing landed on a method that cannot answer the query. Downstream symptom of the pool-construction cluster, not a synthesis-layer defect.

synthesis_terseness.whyItMatters: Correct aggregate data returned but response length and view-type do not expose the underlying detail the buyer expects. Addressable via synthesis-context-selection heuristics; lower priority than the pool cluster.

execution.whyItMatters: Iterative agent loop repeated the same tool call with unsupported filter parameters 10 times without adapting or replanning. Low volume but real adaptation-logic gap; will recur whenever a contributor's schema evolves.

contextBudget.whyItMatters: canonicalBytes=142812 with context_truncated=true. Broad-search tools can blow the context budget; truncation cascades into synthesisBudget and mcpTransport penalties for the same trace. Downstream of the routing cluster.

calibrationNotes: Analyzer determineBottleneckStage heuristic (executionDurationMs/totalDurationMs >= 0.4) is producing measurement artifacts for iterative execution. executionDurationMs is summed across loops while totalDurationMs is wall clock, producing ratios >100% on 9/20 traces. Recommend switching to a true max-stage-elapsed metric or clamping to [0,1] before thresholding.
Analyzer 'selection-or-routing-misses on answerable prompts' flag triggers on 19/20 traces driven by the same broken heuristic; treat as noise until the heuristic is repaired.
mcpTransport low-score on toolCallCount>=8 fires on legitimate broad-scan prompts (q16, q21, q23). Gate the low score on observed transport error rate, not call count.

Only 2/20 traces triggered clarification under clarificationPolicy:auto; the bulk of the pool-construction evidence comes from the single-grounded-interpretation collapse path. Future runs could mirror a subset under clarificationPolicy:return to surface the user-visible failure mode on the same prompt set.
qualitativeSummary: The analyzer paints execution as the weakest stage at 2.1, but that is a measurement artifact of a latency-ratio heuristic that produces >100% bottleneck shares on iterative execution. Raw developer traces show execution is clean on 17/20 prompts (retryCount=0, selfHealCount=0, failureCount=0). The genuinely weakest stage by trace-grounded evidence is synthesis (mean 4.15), driven by three synthesis=1 traces (q5, q9, q20). Those three traces plus the two triggered clarifications (q1, q21) all exhibit one underlying failure: the clarification/pool-construction layer drops the top-fitScore candidate from the visible 3-option pool or from the single-grounded-interpretation collapse and settles on a method with narrower intent coverage. The method-retrieval ranker itself is producing correct fitScores; the bug is downstream of ranking, in the viability/comparison filter that decides which candidates reach comparedOptionIds and which collapse into viableCandidateCount=1 outcomes. Recommended target is the clarification pool-construction layer; synthesis, synthesisBudget, contextBudget, and mcpTransport means should improve as side-effects without direct work on those dimensions. The one genuine non-clarification signal is q16, where the iterative loop repeated the same failing tool call 10 times without replanning - queue this behind the clarification fix as a separate adaptation-logic concern. Measurement confidence is high for clarification (5-trace stable cluster), high for synthesis-as-symptom, low for execution (needs analyzer heuristic repair before it can be scored reliably).

q1.notes: Clarification fired with 3 options. The method-retrieval ranker's top candidate had fitScore=37.55, but the viable pool that reached comparedOptionIds consisted of three methods at materially lower fitScores (top recommended at 23.14, a 62% gap vs the dropped candidate). The top-fit method was a discovery-surface method appropriate for a ranked-discovery prompt; it was filtered out of the viable pool upstream of the clarification judge. In clarificationPolicy:auto the iterative agent recovered and the trace still produced finalScore=4.5, silently passing this failure class through the scoring pipeline. In clarificationPolicy:return (browser) the user would see a semantically-wrong 3-way modal. This is the canonical evidence for the viable_pool_excludes_top_fit_candidate cluster.

q5.notes: Same pool-construction pull-point as q1 surfacing via the single-grounded-interpretation collapse. viableCandidateCount=1 selected a catalog-browse method with an unresolved required param over a trade-history method ranked 2nd by fitScore whose unresolved param was actually present in the prompt. Synthesis honestly reported 'no trades' - downstream symptom.

q9.notes: Top candidate (probability-filter method, fitScore 20.28) was excluded in favor of a keyword-search tool for a numeric-range query. Agent looped 7 times searching for range-filtered results the chosen tool could not produce.

q16.notes: Iterative agent repeated the same tool call with unsupported filter parameters 10 times without replanning. The only genuine execution-layer issue in the 20-trace set. failureCount=10, retryCount=0 - no self-heal triggered.

q19.notes: 182-char response with correct aggregates (743 events, $0 volume) but missing per-event breakdown the buyer expected. Synthesis-terseness cluster.

q20.notes: Pool construction picked a catalog-browse method for a bid-arithmetic query; an efficiency-check method was semantically correct. Market was finalized so arithmetic was unreachable anyway, but synthesis did not adapt to the finalized case with useful context.

q21.notes: Strongest magnitude of the viable_pool_excludes_top_fit_candidate cluster: 0 of 3 offered options fit the 'search by title' intent. The keyword-search method that was semantically correct ranked #1 in candidateSummaries (fitScore 24.48) but was excluded from comparedOptionIds. Recommended option required a single ticker the query does not provide. Also exhibits a clarification label/optionId mismatch (label 'Search Kalshi markets' while recommendedOptionId is an orderbook-lookup method). Iterative agent needed 12 loops (budget 10) to recover and still truncated context at 142KB.

interactionRisks.risk: When pool construction selects a semantically-wrong method, synthesis faithfully reports sparse or empty data and takes the scoring hit. Pure synthesis-layer changes would mask the upstream defect rather than fix it. Fix clarification first, then re-measure synthesis on the same trace set.

Bad pool construction forces the iterative agent into long compensatory loops, consuming step budget (observed at 12 completed vs 10 budgeted) and raising tool-call counts. Execution-layer changes cannot undo the upstream routing error; expect loop counts to drop as a side-effect of the clarification fix.

Bad pool construction pushes the agent into broad fallback searches that can truncate context (single trace at 142KB). Context budget pressure is compensatory, not independent; do not target it standalone this iteration.
High tool-call counts on compensated traces trip the analyzer's call-count proxy for transport; changes here risk double-counting. Prefer fixing the root routing issue.

Iterative loop that repeats a failing tool call (observed on one trace) straddles planner and execution-loop responsibilities. If addressed, both stages must move together. Queue behind the clarification fix.

recommendationForHypothesisGenerator.why: The dominant failure cluster viable_pool_excludes_top_fit_candidate recurs in 5 of 20 traces (25%) across both the clarification-triggered path and the single-grounded-interpretation collapse path. The method-retrieval ranker is producing correct fitScores - in every failing trace the semantically-best method is top-ranked in candidateSummaries. The bug is downstream of ranking: the viability/comparison filter that constructs the visible candidate pool (the inputs to comparedOptionIds and the gate that decides viableCandidateCount=1) is excluding top-ranked candidates, typically when they have unresolved required parameters or broader scope than narrower alternatives. This is the upstream pull-point; synthesis, synthesisBudget, contextBudget, and mcpTransport means should lift as side-effects when it is fixed. Do not target synthesis or execution directly - they are symptoms or measurement artifacts.
likelyCodeLocations: Candidate viability filter that produces the visible pool from the ranker's candidateSummaries - specifically the logic that decides which of the top-K candidates reach comparedOptionIds and which are pruned for parameter unresolvability, scope narrowness, or method-kind mismatch.

Single-grounded-interpretation collapse path that reduces a multi-candidate pool to viableCandidateCount=1 based on decisionSignals like direct_composite_intent_match and exact_identifier_single_tool_overlap.

Clarification judge's pool-construction input boundary, where the mapping from ranked candidates to the 3-option offering is defined (including the label/optionId coherence check that produced the q21 label mismatch).

Parameter-resolution/inference layer that currently excludes candidates when required params are unresolved, even when the query text contains the info (as in the q5 case where a ticker was in the prompt but not matched to the unresolved param field).
guardrails: Preserve exact_identifier_single_tool_overlap and capability_miss_rescued signals that correctly handle 9 clean traces (q2, q3, q4, q7, q8, q11, q17, q22, q23, q24).
Do not regress the single_grounded_interpretation fast-path on exact-identifier prompts; track comparedOptionIds size and content before/after.
Do not up-weight method-name vs method-description tokens in the ranker - the raw trace shows the ranker is already correct. The bug is downstream of ranking.

Re-measure the same 20-trace set under clarificationPolicy:auto after the fix. Additionally rerun a small subset (at minimum the q1 prompt) under clarificationPolicy:return to validate the user-visible modal also improves.
Instrument the delta between candidateSummaries[0] and comparedOptionIds[0] per trace so cluster shrinkage is measurable mechanically between iterations.
Validate the label/optionId coherence of clarification offerings; q21 showed a label that described a different method than the recommendedOptionId.
Confirm the fix is contributor-agnostic by re-running any regression baseline that covers non-Kalshi contributors before declaring success.

successMetrics.primary: Shrink viable_pool_excludes_top_fit_candidate cluster from 5 traces to 0-1 traces.
successMetrics.secondary: Lift synthesis mean from 4.15 toward 4.5+; lift finalScoreMean from 4.02 toward 4.3+; reduce completedStepCount/stepBudget ratio on compensated traces.
successMetrics.guardrailMetric: Do not introduce any new clarification.triggered=true on exact-identifier prompts (q2, q4, q10, q11, q12).
